content.text:
### General settings
set -g base-index 1
set -g escape-time 0
set -g status-position top
set -g key-table vi
set -g mouse on
set -g default-shell /usr/bin/nu
set-option -g focus-events on

# Allow passthrough + environment updates
set -g allow-passthrough on
set -ga update-environment TERM
set -ga update-environment TERM_PROGRAM

# Terminal overrides
set -sa terminal-overrides ",ghostty*:Tc"

### Keybindings
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind v copy-mode
bind -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind -T copy-mode-vi C-v send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-selection-and-cancel
bind b set-option status
bind '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind % split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"


### Plugins (using tpm)
set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
# set -g @plugin 'omerxx/tmux-sessionx'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'

# Session wizard config
# set -g @sessionx-zoxide-mode 'on'
# set -g @sessionx-bind 's'
bind-key "s" display-popup -E -w 81% -h 70% -d '#{pane_current_path}' -T 'Sesh' /usr/bin/nu -c 'tv sesh'
bind -N "last-session (via sesh) " L run-shell "sesh last"

# Resurrect config
set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'
set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @resurrect-save-command-strategy 'ps'

# Continuum config
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '10'

# Configure tmux nvim navigations 
# See: https://github.com/christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator
vim_pattern='(\S+/)?g?\.?(view|l?n?vim?x?|fzf)(diff)?(-wrapped)?'
is_vim="ps -o state= -o comm= -t '#{pane_tty}' \
    | grep -iqE '^[^TXZ ]+ +${vim_pattern}$'"
bind-key -n 'C-h' if-shell "$is_vim" 'send-keys C-h'  'select-pane -L'
bind-key -n 'C-j' if-shell "$is_vim" 'send-keys C-j'  'select-pane -D'
bind-key -n 'C-k' if-shell "$is_vim" 'send-keys C-k'  'select-pane -U'
bind-key -n 'C-l' if-shell "$is_vim" 'send-keys C-l'  'select-pane -R'
tmux_version='$(tmux -V | sed -En "s/^tmux ([0-9]+(.[0-9]+)?).*/\1/p")'
if-shell -b '[ "$(echo "$tmux_version < 3.0" | bc)" = 1 ]' \
    "bind-key -n 'C-\\' if-shell \"$is_vim\" 'send-keys C-\\'  'select-pane -l'"
if-shell -b '[ "$(echo "$tmux_version >= 3.0" | bc)" = 1 ]' \
    "bind-key -n 'C-\\' if-shell \"$is_vim\" 'send-keys C-\\\\'  'select-pane -l'"

bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-h' select-pane -L
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-j' select-pane -D
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-k' select-pane -U
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-l' select-pane -R
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-\' select-pane -l

# pi settings
set -g extended-keys on
set -g extended-keys-format csi-u

# theme
source-file ~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/tmux.conf.theme

### TPM init (keep at bottom)
run '/usr/share/tmux-plugin-manager/tpm'

# Kanagawa for [tmux](https://github.com/tmux/tmux/wiki) [Kanagawa](https://github.com/rebelot/kanagawa.nvim/tree/master) theme for TMUX with silent colors. Forked from [dracula/tmux](https://github.com/dracula/tmux). ![Screenshot](./screenshot.png) ## Install To activate the theme add the following line to your `.tmux.conf`. set -g @plugin 'Nybkox/tmux-kanagawa' For advanced instructions look at [INSTALL.md](https://github.com/Nybkox/tmux-kanagawa/blob/master/INSTALL.md) or check official instructions of [dracula/tmux](https://draculatheme.com/tmux). Just replace all `dracula` occurrences with `kanagawa`. ## Configuration Configuration and options can be found at [draculatheme.com/tmux](https://draculatheme.com/tmux). Just replace all `dracula` occurrences with `kanagawa`. To set the theme flavor (`wave` (default), `lotus` or `dragon`) you can press prefix+T or add the following line to your `.tmux.conf`. Otherwise, the base theme will be set. ``` set -g @kanagawa-theme '' ``` If you want to preserve your emulator background / foreground for window: ``` set -g @kanagawa-ignore-window-colors true ``` **Kanagawa Theme** ![Default Theme](./assets/themes/default.png) **Dragon Theme** ![Dragon Theme](./assets/themes/dragon.png) **Lotus Theme** ![Lotus Theme](./assets/themes/lotus.png) The Kanagawa theme for tmux also supports an extended list of colors from the Kaganawa color palette. For the complete list, refer to [colors](./scripts/colors.sh). ## Features - Support for powerline - Day, date, time, timezone - Current location based on network with temperature and forecast icon (if available) - Network connection status, bandwidth and SSID - SSH session user, hostname and port of active tmux pane - Git branch and status - Battery percentage and AC power connection status - Refresh rate control - CPU usage (percentage or load average) - RAM usage (system and/or tmux server) - GPU usage - Custom status texts from external scripts - GPU VRAM usage - GPU power draw - Color code based on whether a prefix is active or not - List of windows with the current window highlighted - When prefix is enabled, a smiley face turns from green to yellow - When charging, 'AC' is displayed - If forecast information is available, a ☀, ☁, ☂, or ❄ unicode character corresponding with the forecast is displayed alongside the temperature - Info if the Panes are synchronized - Spotify playback (needs the tool spotify-tui installed) - Music Player Daemon status (needs the tool mpc installed) - Playerctl, get current track metadata - Current kubernetes context - Countdown to tmux-continuum save - Current working directory of tmux pane ## Todo - [ ] meaningful / semantic color names ## Compatibility Compatible with macOS and Linux. Tested on tmux 3.1b FreeBSD compatibility is in development ## License [MIT License](./LICENSE)

I checked your tmux config, and the issue is pretty clear:

### Problem
In `~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf` you have:

```tmux
# theme
source-file ~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/tmux.conf.theme
```

and that file currently contains only:

```tmux
set -g @kanagawa-theme dragon
```

That line only sets a **theme option**, but it does **not actually load the Kanagawa theme plugin**.

### Why your top bar looks default now
Your active tmux status is currently:

- `status-left`: `[#{session_name}]`
- `status-right`: continuum save script + pane title + time/date

So tmux is using the normal/default statusline, not the Kanagawa theme.

Also, in your `tmux.conf`, your plugin list is:

```tmux
set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
```

There is **no**:

```tmux
set -g @plugin 'Nybkox/tmux-kanagawa'
```

Even though the plugin files do exist locally in:

`~/.config/tmux/plugins/tmux-kanagawa`

TPM only loads plugins that are declared with `@plugin`.

---

## Most likely cause
At some point, your tmux setup changed from “explicitly loading kanagawa” to only sourcing this tiny theme file from Omarchy:

`~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/tmux.conf.theme`

But that file only sets:

```tmux
set -g @kanagawa-theme dragon
```

So the plugin never runs, and the top bar never gets rebuilt.

---

## Fix
Add the Kanagawa plugin to your tmux plugin list in:

`~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf`

Example:

```tmux
set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
set -g @plugin 'Nybkox/tmux-kanagawa'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
```

Then reload tmux:

```bash
tmux source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf
```

If needed, press TPM install/reload:

- `prefix + I` to install plugins
- or restart tmux

---

## Optional improvement
If you want the theme to actually show the fancier powerline-style top bar, you may also want to set options like:

```tmux
set -g @kanagawa-show-powerline true
set -g @kanagawa-plugins "cpu-usage ram-usage battery network time"
```

Right now your Omarchy theme file only sets the flavor (`dragon`), not the layout/features.

---

## Summary
The theme is not broken because of tmux itself.  
It’s not loading because:

1. `tmux.conf.theme` only sets `@kanagawa-theme dragon`
2. `tmux-kanagawa` is installed locally but **not declared as a tmux plugin**
3. So tmux falls back to your default status bar

If you want, I can patch `home/dot_config/tmux/tmux.conf` for you to re-enable the theme properly.

require("config.lazy")

local lazypath = vim.fn.stdpath("data") .. "/lazy/lazy.nvim"
if not (vim.uv or vim.loop).fs_stat(lazypath) then
  local lazyrepo = "https://github.com/folke/lazy.nvim.git"
  local out = vim.fn.system({ "git", "clone", "--filter=blob:none", "--branch=stable", lazyrepo, lazypath })
  if vim.v.shell_error ~= 0 then
    vim.api.nvim_echo({
      { "Failed to clone lazy.nvim:\n", "ErrorMsg" },
      { out, "WarningMsg" },
      { "\nPress any key to exit..." },
    }, true, {})
    vim.fn.getchar()
    os.exit(1)
  end
end

I found it.

### What’s happening
Your Neovim config expects this file to exist:

`~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/theme.lua`

And it does exist now, but as a symlink:

```text
~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/theme.lua -> /home/allan/.config/omarchy/current/theme/neovim.lua
```

So the real problem is that the symlink points to:

`/home/allan/...`

but your current user/home is:

`/home/allank/...`

That stale symlink target is broken.

### Why this happened
Looks like this config was originally created on a machine or account named `allan`, and after moving to `allank`, the symlink didn’t get updated.

---

## Fix
You should recreate the symlink so it points to the correct file:

```bash
ln -sf /home/allank/.config/omarchy/current/theme/neovim.lua ~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/theme.lua
```

Or equivalently:

```bash
ln -sf ~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/neovim.lua ~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/theme.lua
```

After that, Neovim should stop throwing the error.

---

## Extra note
Your theme file itself is valid and contains:

```lua

  {
    "LazyVim/LazyVim",
    opts = {
      colorscheme = "kanagawa",
    },
  },
}
```

So this is not a Lua/theme issue — just a broken symlink path.
